All right, so the final position that you will most likely hire at your gym, which will be a dedicated front desk person, meaning all they do is work the front desk. And so this is more of a luxury position to have for your managers or whoever was running the front desk before. So typically before you hire a dedicated front desk person, you want to have one of your managers, whether it's the retention manager, the challenge manager, the ops manager, not the sales manager because they're going to constantly be leaving. But one of those three, or maybe if you have trainers that don't mind doing front desk hours in between their sessions, however you have it set up, typically you'll have one of them behind the front desk greeting members by their first name, all of that stuff, and keeping things organized, checking people in the classes, making copies of paperwork, signing people in for their orientation, maybe weighing them in, things like that. But at some point your managers will be so busy doing what they do best, which is retention, challenges, working with challengers specifically, sales, all of those things to where it doesn't make sense to have their attention be taken up by people that just want to talk, which happens all the time at the gym. You'll have people that just finished their workout and want to explain what they liked about it, people talking about their life, hanging out at the front desk, just chatting. Members chatting with each other and then that kind of leaks over into asking questions of the front desk person. You guys know how it goes, but most of these managers, you're paying $15, $20 an hour to do high level stuff when their attention could be getting absorbed by somebody that you could pay $10 an hour. That's when you hire the dedicated front desk person is when your managers are not getting everything done because there's so many, the volume, you've been scaling for a while now. There's a lot of members at the gym, there's a lot of challengers at the gym, your sales people are crushing it, your owners are getting really good at lead gen from all the time that they have freed up from stuff they don't have to do. They're not doing it because they're so good at lead gen, it's flooding the gym and so these managers are real busy, which is awesome. You want your managers busy because they're fulfilling everything at a really high level, but that's when it can become problematic if they're sitting behind the front desk while they're working because that attention is going to get grabbed. Then you want to split them off somewhere else. If you have another office somewhere, at first I stuck two of my managers in the same office and threw a couple desks in there and they just went to work and really crushed it. Or you could have them behind the front desk as well, but maybe if it's a large enough area, you have somebody else that is just dedicated to some of the more easier tasks to do. At that point, this is all about protecting the attention of your higher level leaders. You bring that person out, you pay them $10 an hour or something relatively cheap and they just sit up there and you don't want them doing nothing and twiddling their thumbs and you don't want them to pay them for doing nothing. There's a ton of small tasks like stocking protein shakes and cleaning and all of that stuff. In fact, there's a whole training portal that you can download and make it your own. It's a ClickFunnels share portal. Make it your own, switch out all the videos so you have this perfect training for your front desk and exactly how they're going to do it at your gym. There's a 4Rs document that you can download and this overview summary of their daily tasks, you can download and print off for them as well. This is probably the final position that you'll have and once you do hire this, you should be fully staffed at your gym and you should be really crushing it. This is a really fun position because then everybody comes in and they really love what they're doing every day and it's great to build a strong culture. Find somebody that's super friendly but can control the communication so they're not just up there chatting the whole time. Hopefully you guys do well with this and we can move on to the next part.
